Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanusc renatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminariga sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Small cave (4 m long, 1 m high) with abundant Caryophyllia smithii on the roof and walls. Also sheets of thin encrusting sponges, especially Aplysilla sulfurea (both colour morphs), small patches of Thymosia guernei and Galathea strigosa in cracks and crevices in the roof.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park on bedrock with a dense understory turf of red algae (mainly Plocamium cartilagineum and Delesseria sanguinea), bryozoans (mainly Bugula flabellata, Crisia eburnea and Scrupocellaria spp.) and sponges (mainly Esperiopsis fucorum and Dysidea fragilis). Dyphasia rosacea was on the kelp stipes.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Bedrock gullies and ridges with kelp park with an understory of sparse sponge- Polymastia boletiformis and Hemimycale columella, coralline crusts and sparse red algae. Also patches of Balanus crenatus and Crisiids.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella cilaita and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Overhang in a 8 m long tunnel formed by boulders, big enough to swim through. Dominant species were massive growths of white Pachymatisma johnstonia. Between these are dense clumps of Tubularia indivisa, sheets of Corynactis viridis and underlying Balanus crenatus were the dominant spceies.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antenninah and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.

A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspala ramosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Very tide-swept circalittoral cobbles with Sertuaria argentea, patches of Polyclinum aurantium, Aplidium densum, Nucella lapillus and abundant Urticina felina. Large patches of Tubularia indivisa.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Very tide-swept circalittoral cobbles with Sertularia argentea, patches of Polyclinum aurantium, Aplidium densum, Nucella lapillus and abundant Urticina felina. Large patches of Tubularia indivisa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. adn bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Boulder overhang with Pachymatisma johnnstonia and a variety of encrusting sponges including Dercitus bucklandi. Other characteristic species were the hydroid Diphasia rosacea, Corynactis viridis and the cup coral Caryophyllia inornata.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Tideswept circalittoral bedrock pinnacle with a dense short turf of a mixture of Tubularia spp., Mytilus edulis, Polyclinum aurantium, Balanus crenatus, Nemertesia antennina and short bushy hydroids. Also Alcyonium digitatum and sparse sponges.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria cgovering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Exposed lower infralittoral bedrock with a Laminaria hyperborea park. This was not surveyed in detail but a red algae understory was present along with red algae on kelp stipes. The predominant red alga was Plocamium cartilagineum.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Vertical bedrock. Sponge and bryozoan turf dominated this biotope. Species include Pachymatisma johstonia, Dysidea fragilis and an orange sponge. Turf species include Bugula turbinata and Crisia denticulata. The sponge Dercitus bucklandii was present. Plants of Plocamium cartilagineum. There were patches of Corynactis viridis.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. A variety of sponges were also present incsluding Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Diphasia rosacea, Balanus crenatus, sparse bryozoans, small tufts of red algae and coralline crusts characterised areas of bedrock where tidal streams were turbulent and accelerated.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cuprerssina and Diphasia rosacea.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Bedrock gullies and ridges with kelp park with an understory of sparse sponge- Polymastia boletiformis and Hemimycale columella, coralline crusts and sparse red algae. Also patches of Balanus crenatus and Crisiids.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Vertical bedrock. Sponge and bryozoan turf dominated this biotope. Species include Pachymatisma johstonia, Dysidea fragilis and an orange sponge. Turf species include Bugula turbinata and Crisia denticulata. The sponge Dercitus bucklandii was present. Plants of Plocamium cartilagineum. There were patches of Corynactis viridis.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Tideswept circalittoral bedrock pinnacle with a dense short turf of a mixture of Tubularia spp., Mytilus edulis, Polyclinum aurantium, Balanus crenatus, Nemertesia antennina and short bushy hydroids. Also Alcyonium digitatum and sparse sponges.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Boulder overhang with Pachymatisma johnnstonia and a variety of encrusting sponges including Dercitus bucklandi. Other characteristic species were the hydroid Diphasia rosacea, Corynactis viridis and the cup coral Caryophyllia inornata.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Also large numbers of sponge, particularly Polymastia boletifomis, Hemimycale columella and Tethya aurantium. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata adn Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park with a mixed understory turf of bryozoans (mainly Bicellariella ciliata and Crisia denticulata), sponges (predominantly Esperiopsis fucorum and red algae(abundant Plocamium cartilagineum). Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: A large limestone outcrop. This description is restricted to an overhang. This was dominated by Pachymatisma johnstonia, Corynactis viridis and Polycarpa rustica. The cup coral Caryophyllia inornata occurred right under the overhang.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Bedrock gullies and ridges with kelp park with an understory of sparse sponge- Polymastia boletiformis and Hemimycale columella, coralline crusts and sparse red algae. Also patches of Balanus crenatus and Crisiids.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Boulder overhang with Pachymatisma johnnstonia and a variety of encrusting sponges including Dercitus bucklandi. Other characteristic species were the hydroid Diphasia rosacea, Corynactis viridis and the cup coral Caryophyllia inornata.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Vertical rock faces and gully walls with dense Tubularia indivisa, and patches of Corynactis viridis, sponges and hydroids. Also clusters of Aplidium densum and Polyclinium aurantium and patches of limestone (?) covered in Polycarpa scuba
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Exposed lower infralittoral bedrock with a Laminaria hyperborea park. This was not surveyed in detail but a red algae understory was present along with red algae on kelp stipes. The predominant red alga was Plocamium cartilagineum.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper.This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Lower circalittoral very exposed, tide-swept bedrock with a dense turf of crisiids, Cellaria spp., sponges including Axinella dissimilis, Tethya aurantium and large Pachymatisma johnstonia, large patches of Gymnangium montagui and patches of Flustra foliacea. Also Alcyonium digitatum and large patches of Corynactis viridis amongst the hydroid/bryozoan turf.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Vertical rock faces and gully walls with dense Tubularia indivisa, and patches of Corynactis viridis, sponges and hydroids. Also clusters of Aplidium densum and Polyclinium aurantium and patches of limestone (?) covered in Polycarpa scuba

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Hydroids, ascidians and sparse algae on silty boulders in the upper circalittoral. Balanus crenatus relatively dense with patches of Morchellium argus and tufts of erect bryozoans and hydroids.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Lower circalittoral bouledrs, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Lower circalittoral boulders, cobbles and pebbles with superabundant Alcyonium digitatum and a hydroid and bryozoan turf, predominantly Bugula spp., Flustra foliacea, Cellaria fistulosa, Nemertesia antennina and Sertularella gayi. A variety of sponges were also present including Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Silty boulders and cobbmles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Silty boulders and cobbles in the upper circalittoral with dense Balanus crenatus, Morchellium argus, Chartella papyracea, Bugula spp. and bryozoan crusts and Dysidea fragilis.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Silty lower infralittoral boulders. Dominant species included Plocamium cartilagineum, Phylpohora crispa and Drachiella heterocarpa. Animals included Balanus crenatus and Dendrodoa grossularia covering the silty rock. Others included Dysidea fragilis, Morchellium argus and Hemimycale columella.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Silty lower infralittoral boulders. Dominant species included Plocamium cartilagineum, Phylophora crispa and Drachiella heterocarpa. Animals included Balanus crenatus and Dendrodoa grossularia covering the silty rock. Others included Dysidea fragilis, Morchellium argus and Hemimycale columella.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix iwth Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

Lower infralittoral boulders and cobbles wth Laminaria hyperborea on the larger boulders and a dense turf of red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa, hydroids including Sertularia argentea, Halecium spp. and Aglaopheonia spp. and bryozoans.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Lower infralittoral boulders and cobbles with Laminaria hyperborea on the larger boulders and a dense turf of red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa, hydroids including Sertularia argentea, Halecium spp. and Aglaopheonia spp. and bryozoans.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Boulder overhang with Pachymatisma johnnstonia and a variety of encrusting sponges including Dercitus bucklandi. Other characteristic species were the hydroid Diphasia rosacea, Corynactis viridis and the cup coral Caryophyllia inornata.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Kelp forest on undulating bedrock. Characteristic species were Laminaria hyperborea and Plocamium cartilagineum. Algae were not well developed. Animals included Sabellaria spinulosa, Balanus crenatus and Esperiopsis fucorum. Of interest was the ascidian Aplidium densum.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Overhang in the lower infralittoral formed by boulders. Walls dominated by Chartella papyracea and patches of the sponge Dysidea fragilis and an orange encrusting sponge. Small barnacles and a fuzz of Obelia spp. dominated the rock beneath. Some plants of Plocamium cartilagineum and Heterosiphonia plumosa were present.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Tideswept circalittoral bedrock pinnacle with a dense short turf of a mixture of Tubularia spp., Mytilus edulis, Polyclinum aurantium, Balanus crenatus, Nemertesia antennina and short bushy hydroids. Also Alcyonium digitatum and sparse sponges.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): 1997 MNCR Bardsey Island and SW Lleyn Peninsula sublittoral survey

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Partially recorded kelp forest on a bedrock shelf above a wall with red algae, sponges and colonial ascidians.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and corallkine crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Boulders, cobbles and pebbles in a coarse sand matrix with Balanus crenatus and coralline crusts throughout, Sertularia argentea, other hydroids and short bryozoans on larger boulders with red algae including Plocamium cartilagineum, Delesseria sanguinea and Corallina officinalis.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Areas of overhang on a wall and large boulder holes were extensive throughout the site with dense Corynactis viridis and Caryophyllia inornata. Numerous species of sponge were also present and a thin turf of bryozoans in patches.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Exposed lower infralittoral Laminaria hyperborea park with a mixed understory turf of bryozoans (mainly Bicellariella ciliata and Crisia denticulata), sponges (predominantly Esperiopsis fucorum and red algae(abundant Plocamium cartilagineum). Also present were abundant Morchellium argus.

Between these are dense clumps of Tubularia indivisa, sheets of Corynactis viridis and underlying Balanus crenatus were the dominant species.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Hydroids, ascidians and sparse algae on silty boulders in the upper circalittoral. Balanus crenatus relatively dense with patches of Morchellium argus and tufts of erect bryozoans and hydroids.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Dense turf of Tubularia indivisa also with patches of Balanus crenatus. Other conspicuous species included Halichondria panicea, Sagartia elegans and Crisia denticulata. The habitat was the upper surfaces of tideswept rock- very large boulders in fact.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Boulders and cobbles in the infralittoral were surrounded by and scoured by coarse sand. The algae were dominated by ephemeral or sand scour resistant species such as Laminaria saccharina, Halidrys siliquosa, Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa. Sabellaria spinulosa frequently found beneath smothering sand.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Small cave (4 m long, 1 m high) with abundant Caryophyllia smithii on the roof and walls. Also sheets of thin encrusting sponges, especially Aplysilla sulfurea (both colour morphs), small patches of Thymosia guernei and Galathea strigosa in cracks and crevices in the roof.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Laminaria hyperborea forest with a dense understory of Plocamium cartilagineum, coralline crusts, Phycodrys rubens on the kelp stipes, colonial ascidians and bryozoans.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Also large numbers of sponge, particularly Poymastia boletiformis, Hemimycale columella and Tethya aurantium. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Lower infralittoral bedrock and boulders with sparse kelp and foliose red algae. Also large numbers of sponge, particularly Polymastia boletiformis, Hemimycale columella and Tethya aurantium. Polycarpa rustica/Polycarpa pomaria covering large areas of rock with a few Ascidiella scabra. Other colonial ascidians, particularly Polyclinum aurantium and Aplidium densum collectively common.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): Vertical wall of 2m height with a dense turf of Chartella papyracea and Crisia spp. with edges of fissures colonised by patches of Aglaophenia spp. Dense clumps of Parazoanthus axinella limited to the upper 2/3 rds of the wall.

Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefy)n: A single bedrock/large boulder outcrop was studied. The rock was covered in Flustra foliacea (with the hydroid Laomedea flexuosa). Also conspicuous was Balanus crenatus and the ascidian Morchellium argus.
Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn): A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.
